On October 22nd, 2023, at about 9:10 PM, I was walking my dog along the south bank of the Mill Creek behind 1450 Cottonwood Drive when I noticed a steady gray-white discharge coming out of a six-inch pipe set into the bank. The pipe is partially hidden behind a stand of bamboo. The discharge had a strong solvent smell, almost like paint thinner. The water around the outfall was discolored for about thirty feet downstream and there were three dead fish floating just past the discharge point. I took two short videos on my phone and noted the GPS coordinates as 41.4827 north, 81.6914 west. The pipe appears to come from the back of the auto-body shop at 1442 Cottonwood. I called the state environmental hotline the next morning and was told to file a written report.
